title: cistus atriplicifolius subsp. macrocalycinus
intro: Cistus atriplicifolius subsp. macrocalycinus, also known as Rock Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its greyish-green, fleshy leaves and vibrant pink to purple flowers. Traditionally, Rock Rose has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing stress and anxiety, and supporting the immune system. Rock Rose can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and calmness.
